data.title: In September, Warner Bros. Discovery leads S&P 500 as fastest-rising stock due to potential merger and acquisition news.
data.description: Stocks have experienced a significant boost following potential merger and acquisition reports. Warner Bros. Discovery has emerged as the top performer in September, leading the S&P 500. The surprising standout as September's fastest-rising stock in the S&P 500 showcases the impact of the merger and acquisition news on market dynamics.

data.title: CyberBay 2025 Report warns of escalating cyber threats, urges action. Insights from IT pros in report. Download at cyberbay.org.
data.description: In Tampa Bay, Florida, cyber threats are on the rise in both scale and complexity. Despite this increasing danger, our defense mechanisms are fragmented, costly, and inaccessible to many organizations. The recently published 2025 CyberBay Report, which draws from input provided by numerous IT and cybersecurity experts, highlights this concerning trend and emphasizes the critical need for immediate action from the private sector, educators, and cyber defense professionals. The report can be accessed for download at https://cyberbay.org/survey/.

data.title: Intellia Therapeutics presents positive Phase 1 data for Nexiguran Ziclumeran in ATTR amyloidosis, published in NEJM.
data.description: Intellia Therapeutics, Inc., a prominent gene editing company listed on NASDAQ as NTLA, shared updated information from the Phase 1 study of investigational drug nexiguran ziclumeran (nex-z) at the 5th International ATTR Amyloidosis Annual Meeting in Baveno, Italy. The study focuses on treating hereditary ATTR amyloidosis with polyneuropathy (ATTRv-PN). The details were presented on September 25, 2025, and are now accessible on intelliatx.com's Scientific Publications & Presentations page. The findings were also published in the New England Journal of Medicine.

data.title: Nasdaq fell over 1%, with Dow down 0.50% at 45,889.75 and Nasdaq down 1.38% at 22,187.39. Initial jobless claims dropped to 218,000.
data.description: This morning, U.S. stocks saw a decline, led by the Nasdaq Composite dropping over 1%. The Dow traded at 45,889.75, a decrease of 0.50%, while the NASDAQ fell 1.38% to 22,187.39. The S&P 500 also saw a decrease of 0.94% to 6,575.35. Consumer staples shares rose by 0.5%, but communication services stocks fell by 1.5%. Initial jobless claims dropped by 14,000 to 218,000 in the third week of September, beating market estimates of 235,000. Notable stock movements included PepGen Inc. (NASDAQ: PEPG) shares surging 107% to $5.51 after announcing a $100 million public offering, and SciSparc Ltd. (NASDAQ: SPRC) rising 79% to $6.86.
